_WARNING: SYNTHETIC SAMPLE — every company, deal, trial result, and source in this file is INVENTED to exercise the schema. Not research output. Never publish, never cite, never copy into issues/. A real issue.json must not carry the _FIXTURE key.

tldr_headline.title: Aventis Onco swallows Helix Therapeutics for $9.4B, betting the farm on antibody-drug conjugates
tldr_headline.summary: The all-cash deal is the largest oncology M&A of 2026 so far and signals big pharma's scramble to buy into next-generation ADC platforms before patents on first-generation payloads erode.

tldr_bullets.text: Aventis Onco acquires Helix Therapeutics for $9.4B, its biggest ADC bet to date.
Meridian Bio's Phase 3 KRAS-G12D readout hits its primary endpoint; overall survival data still immature.
FDA finalizes accelerated-approval confirmatory-trial guidance, tightening post-marketing timelines.
Two China-originated bispecifics get out-licensed to Western pharma in a single week, extending the licensing wave.

tracked_watchlist.name: Helix Therapeutics
tracked_watchlist.summary: Aventis Onco agreed to acquire Helix for $9.4B all-cash ($47/share, a 58% premium), gaining Helix's site-specific ADC conjugation platform and its lead HER3-targeting candidate HLX-201 (Phase 2).

research_angle.take: This validates our Q2 thesis that ADC conjugation chemistry — not the antibody — is the scarce asset. Aventis paid a platform premium, not a pipeline premium; HLX-201 alone doesn't justify $9.4B. Expect Roche and AstraZeneca to chase the two remaining independent conjugation platforms within two quarters.

tracked_watchlist.name: Meridian Bio
tracked_watchlist.summary: Topline Phase 3 (MERIDIAN-301) in KRAS-G12D-mutant pancreatic cancer met its primary PFS endpoint (HR 0.61). Overall survival data immature; full results promised at ESMO 2026.

research_angle.take: PFS win is real but the Street is over-reading it — pancreatic PFS has a weak history of translating to OS, and the HR confidence interval (0.44–0.85) is wide. We stay neutral until ESMO OS data. A licensing deal is more likely than an outright buyout at current $2.1B market cap.

tracked_watchlist.name: Northwind Oncology
tracked_watchlist.summary: Closed a $180M Series C led by Sequoia Bio to advance its allogeneic CAR-T platform into Phase 1. Post-money valuation reported at ~$720M.

research_angle.take: Allogeneic CAR-T funding is thawing after two winters, but Northwind's differentiation is thin versus at least four better-capitalized rivals. We watch, don't chase.

no_news.name: Atlas Immuno
no_news.note: Quiet since IND clearance in May.

critic_catches.claim: Reported that Vantage Bio raised a $300M Series D this cycle.
critic_catches.rejection_reason: Provenance failure: the only source traces to a June 2025 Endpoints article recycled by an aggregator; no 2026 filing or PR corroborates a new raise.

open_threads.name: Helix Therapeutics
open_threads.thread: Aventis deal antitrust review (FTC second request possible)

new_on_radar.name: Kestrel Tx
new_on_radar.what_they_do: Emerging-from-stealth startup with an in-vivo CAR-T platform (lipid-nanoparticle delivery of CAR mRNA), skipping ex-vivo manufacturing entirely.
new_on_radar.why_we_care: In-vivo CAR-T would collapse the cost and logistics moat that protects incumbent cell-therapy players — directly on-thesis for our cell-therapy-durability worldview.

new_on_radar.name: Helios Diagnostics
new_on_radar.what_they_do: MRD (minimal residual disease) liquid-biopsy company; raised a $95M Series B.
new_on_radar.why_we_care: MRD testing is becoming the gatekeeper for adjuvant-therapy decisions; a proxy for where oncology trial design is heading.

new_on_radar.name: BioCatalyst Partners
new_on_radar.what_they_do: New $1.2B oncology-focused crossover fund launched by two ex-Aventis dealmakers.
new_on_radar.why_we_care: Signals institutional capital rotating back into oncology; the founders' network predicts which private assets get pulled forward.

themes_and_signals.signal: Two ADC-platform acquisitions in six weeks (Helix now, plus Conjugate Bio in June) suggest the independent-conjugation-platform window is closing.

themes_and_signals.theme: China licensing wave broadens beyond antibodies
themes_and_signals.signal: Out-licensing this cycle includes bispecifics and an ADC, not just naked antibodies — Western pharma is now sourcing whole modalities from Chinese biotechs.

elsewhere_on_frontier.move: Announced a $500M milestone-laden option deal with a European TIL-therapy startup, its first move into tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes.
elsewhere_on_frontier.why_it_matters: Confirms big-pharma appetite for solid-tumor cell therapy is broadening past CAR-T.

elsewhere_on_frontier.move: Reported positive Phase 2 data for its oral SERD in HR+ breast cancer, pressuring the injectable-SERD incumbents.
elsewhere_on_frontier.why_it_matters: Oral convenience could reshape the endocrine-therapy standard of care.

method.paywall_policy: Paywalled facts cited via secondary coverage + link, flagged 'primary paywalled — assess manually'; ~1-week latency accepted.
method.notes: All researchers ran read-only (permission-enforced). Coverage window 2026-07-13 → 2026-07-16.

blocking_findings.finding: Uncorroborated Vantage Bio Series D claim (stale provenance).
blocking_findings.resolution: Removed from watchlist; published as a critic catch in Quiet This Cycle.

advisory_findings.finding: Meridian OS confidence interval not stated in first synthesis draft.
Elsewhere-on-Frontier could have covered a Merck ADC readout also reported this cycle.
advisory_findings.resolution: Noted; below inclusion threshold this cycle.

thesis_changelog.change: Raised conviction from medium to high after second platform acquisition in six weeks.
Added in-vivo CAR-T (Kestrel Tx) as a named disruption risk to the incumbent-moat sub-thesis.
